stages.notes: characterization/: 4 files / 30 tests (store 15, Counter 5, TodoList 8, wiring 2) — all green against original/

decisions.rationale: R1 resolved without a source change: tests get store isolation via vi.resetModules() + dynamic re-import per test, not an added `export const reducer`. No original/ or migrated/ source touched to make tests possible.

characterization/ toolchain mirrors the established dual-target harness (vitest+jsdom+@testing-library/react, TARGET=original|migrated via '@app' alias) from the prior react-redux-realworld-example-app run, scaled down (no router) for this fixture's smaller surface.

Step 2 completed on resume: dep bump to react-redux@8.1.3/redux@4.2.1 found pre-applied but unverified; verification exposed a dual-React "Invalid hook call" failure (13/30), fixed by installing @testing-library/{react,dom,jest-dom} as devDependencies in migrated/ per the dual-target harness design — test tooling only, no runtime dep or source change.

Step 3: store.js left byte-identical — optional export const reducer NOT added; testgen already achieved test isolation via vi.resetModules(), so the export would be an API-surface change with no consumer (rule 2).

Steps 4-5: Counter/TodoList converted class+connect -> function components with useSelector/useDispatch (+useState for TodoList). Preserved exactly: R2 no-trim falsy guard, R6 dispatch-before-clear ordering, R3 key={i} index keys. Selectors return the same primitive/array slices as the old mapStateToProps.

testGates.command: npm run test:original && npm run test:migrated
testGates.summary: Final gate: 30/30 green TARGET=original, 30/30 green TARGET=migrated; sweep found no connect/React.Component/bind(this) in migrated/src
